parameters.jsonBody:
={{ JSON.stringify({ "contents": [ { "parts": [ { "text": "You are an expert at extracting structured data from NYC Police Accident Reports (MV-104AN form).

Analyze the provided police report image and extract the following information into a JSON object.
Be precise and extract the data exactly as it appears in the document.

Extract:
{
  "report_number": "The Accident No. / Complaint Number (e.g. MV-2018-078-002001)",
  "accident_date": "Date in YYYY-MM-DD format from Box 1 (Month/Day/Year)",
  "accident_day_of_week": "Day of week from Box 1",
  "accident_time": "Military time from Box 1",
  "num_vehicles": "Number of vehicles involved",
  "num_injured": "Number of injured persons (integer) - CRITICAL for conditional logic",
  "num_killed": "Number killed",
  
  "vehicle1_driver": {
    "full_name": "Driver name as printed on license (LAST, FIRST format)",
    "first_name": "First name only",
    "last_name": "Last name only",
    "license_number": "License ID Number",
    "license_state": "State of license",
    "date_of_birth": "DOB in YYYY-MM-DD format",
    "sex": "M or F",
    "address": "Full street address",
    "city": "City or Town",
    "state": "State",
    "zip_code": "Zip code"
  },
  
  "vehicle1": {
    "plate_number": "Registration plate number",
    "plate_state": "State of registration",
    "year": "Vehicle year",
    "make": "Vehicle make (manufacturer)",
    "type": "Vehicle type (sedan, SUV, etc.)",
    "insurance_code": "Insurance code"
  },
  
  "vehicle2_driver": {
    "full_name": "Driver name as printed on license",
    "first_name": "First name only",
    "last_name": "Last name only",
    "license_number": "License ID Number",
    "license_state": "State of license",
    "date_of_birth": "DOB in YYYY-MM-DD format if available",
    "sex": "M or F",
    "address": "Full street address",
    "city": "City or Town",
    "state": "State",
    "zip_code": "Zip code"
  },
  
  "vehicle2": {
    "plate_number": "Registration plate number",
    "plate_state": "State of registration",
    "year": "Vehicle year",
    "make": "Vehicle make",
    "type": "Vehicle type"
  },
  
  "accident_location": {
    "borough": "BRONX, KINGS, NEW YORK, QUEENS, or RICHMOND",
    "road": "Road on which accident occurred",
    "intersecting_street": "Intersecting street",
    "full_location": "Combined location description (road at intersecting street, borough)",
    "latitude": "GPS latitude if available",
    "longitude": "GPS longitude if available"
  },
  
  "accident_description": "Full text from Officer's Notes / Accident Description (Box 30)",
  
  "involved_persons": [
    {
      "role": "A, B, C, D etc.",
      "name": "Full name (LAST, FIRST format)",
      "age": "Age if available",
      "sex": "M or F"
    }
  ],
  
  "officer": {
    "name": "Officer name",
    "rank": "Rank",
    "badge_or_tax_id": "Tax ID number",
    "precinct": "Precinct number",
    "review_date": "Date/Time Reviewed"
  },
  
  "filing_info": {
    "filed_date": "Filing date from header",
    "index_number": "Index number from header",
    "nyscef_doc_number": "NYSCEF DOC number"
  },
  
  "_computed": {
    "client_name": "First name + Last name of Vehicle 1 driver (our client/plaintiff)",
    "defendant_name": "First name + Last name of Vehicle 2 driver",
    "client_pronoun": "his or her based on Vehicle 1 driver sex",
    "statute_of_limitations_date": "accident_date + 8 years in YYYY-MM-DD format",
    "has_injuries": true/false based on num_injured > 0
  }
}

IMPORTANT RULES:
1. Vehicle 1 driver is always the CLIENT (plaintiff)
2. Vehicle 2 driver is always the DEFENDANT  
3. Names in the report are in "LAST, FIRST" format - extract both formats
4. If a field is not readable or not present, use null
5. The accident_description should capture the FULL officer's notes text
6. For the _computed section, derive values from extracted data
7. Dates should be in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD)
8. For pedestrian cases (FAUSTO_CASTILLO type), Vehicle 2 may be a pedestrian - note this" }, { "inlineData": { "mimeType": "application/pdf", "data": $json.body.pdf_base64 } } ] } ], "generationConfig": { "responseMimeType": "application/json" } }) }}

    "latitude": "GPS latitude if available",
    "longitude": "GPS longitude if available"
  },

  "involved_persons": [
    {
      "role": "A, B, C, D etc.",
      "name": "Full name (LAST, FIRST format)",
      "age": "Age if available",
      "sex": "M or F"
    }
  ],
  
  "officer": {
    "name": "Officer name",
    "rank": "Rank",
    "badge_or_tax_id": "Tax ID number",
    "precinct": "Precinct number",
    "review_date": "Date/Time Reviewed"
  },
  
  "filing_info": {
    "filed_date": "Filing date from header",
    "index_number": "Index number from header",
    "nyscef_doc_number": "NYSCEF DOC number"
  },
  
  "_computed": {
    "client_name": "First name + Last name of Vehicle 1 driver (our client/plaintiff)",
    "defendant_name": "First name + Last name of Vehicle 2 driver",
    "client_pronoun": "his or her based on Vehicle 1 driver sex",
    "statute_of_limitations_date": "accident_date + 8 years in YYYY-MM-DD format",
    "has_injuries": true/false based on num_injured > 0
  }
}

IMPORTANT RULES:
1. Vehicle 1 driver is always the CLIENT (plaintiff)
2. Vehicle 2 driver is always the DEFENDANT  
3. Names in the report are in "LAST, FIRST" format - extract both formats
4. If a field is not readable or not present, use null
5. The accident_description should capture the FULL officer's notes text
6. For the _computed section, derive values from extracted data
7. Dates should be in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD)
8. For pedestrian cases (FAUSTO_CASTILLO type), Vehicle 2 may be a pedestrian - note this" }, { "inlineData": { "mimeType": "application/pdf", "data": $json.body.pdf_base64 } } ] } ], "generationConfig": { "responseMimeType": "application/json" } }) }}
